name: Cloudflare MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Cloudflare MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Cloudflare's platform — managing Workers, KV storage, D1 databases, R2 buckets, DNS records, firewall rules, and other Cloudflare services.
use_cases: Deploying and managing Cloudflare Workers from infrastructure agents
Querying and updating DNS records for domain management agents
Managing Cloudflare KV namespaces and D1 databases from data agents
Configuring firewall rules and WAF settings from security agents
Monitoring analytics and request logs from observability agents
Managing R2 object storage from data pipeline agents
not_for: Teams using AWS CloudFront, Fastly, or other CDN providers
best_when: An agent needs to manage Cloudflare Workers, DNS, storage, or edge infrastructure — especially in Cloudflare-native architectures.
avoid_when: You're using AWS, GCP, Azure, or another cloud provider for edge/CDN.

auth.notes: Cloudflare API tokens with granular permissions per resource type. API keys also supported but tokens preferred. Token scopes map directly to Cloudflare resource permissions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 100K Workers requests/day, 1GB KV storage, limited D1
pricing.paid_tiers: Workers Paid: $5/month + $0.50/million requests; D1, R2, KV usage-based
pricing.notes: Generous free tier for development. MCP server is open source. Cloudflare Workers and edge products have their own pricing tiers.

performance.rate_limits: API: 1,200 requests/5 minutes per token; varies by endpoint

known_agent_gotchas: Account ID required for most API calls — agents must discover it first
Zone ID vs account ID — many endpoints need zone-level scope, not account-level
Workers deployment requires wrangler config or direct API — MCP abstracts this
Rate limits are per-token and vary by endpoint type
D1 is serverless SQLite — agents must use SQL syntax, not ORM patterns
KV is eventually consistent — agents must account for propagation delays
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Granular API token scopes per resource type. SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, FedRAMP. Enterprise-grade Cloudflare security infrastructure.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Granular token scopes. SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, FedRAMP. Enterprise Cloudflare security.
